segments.text:
The Trp Operon
enzymes needed to synthesize tryptophan

Regulation of transcription initiation
Low free trp = trp synthesis needed  RNA pol initiates transcription

Regulation of transcription initiation
Adequate free trp    transcription is repressed
trp repressor protein is allosteric
trp = co-repressor
is this positive or negative regulation?
Trp operon leader (5’ UTR) = second regulatory region

Trp operon leader
leader polypeptide coding sequence
attenuator region

met   lys    ala    ile   phe   val   leu    lys    gly    trp    trp    arg   thr   ser  (stop)
CAAUGAAAGCAAUUUUCGUACUGAAAGGUUGGUGGCGCACUUCCUGAAA
Trp operon Leader Polypeptide coding sequence
measures availability of charged trp-tRNAs

adequate trp-tRNAs – stop transcription here
low on charged trp-tRNAs – transcribe whole operon

Possible stem-loops in attenuator region
an intrinsic terminator
not a terminator

Coupled transcription-translation
ribosome begins translation
while RNA pol is transcribing attenuator

low trp-tRNAs – ribosome stalls at trp codons
transcription continues
first stem-loop in attenuator forms

Adequate charged trp-tRNAs – ribosome does not stall
ribosome blocks formation of first stem-loop
terminator stem-loop forms

low trp-tRNAs
adequate trp-tRNAs
modulates frequency
of transcription in
response to need for
trp to charge tRNAs

Other leader polypeptides
Attenuation ONLY works in prokaryotes!

Steps at which gene expression can be regulated
DNA
primary transcript
functional mRNA
polypeptide (primary translation product)
processed protein
active protein
transcription
RNA processing, nuclear export
(eukaryotes only)
translation
mRNA stability
protein processing
subunit assembly, cofactor interactions
protein targeting (eukaryotes)
protein modifications (activation/inhibition)
protein stability

most small subunits start at 5’ end
~50% of ribosomes dissociate per stop codon
exonucleases generally 3’  5’

Regulation of protein activity – feedback inhibition
End product is allosteric inhibitor of first step
